title: The Folly Of Being Comforted
content:
One that is ever kind said yesterday:
"Your well-beloved's hair has threads of grey,
And little shadows come about her eyes;
Time can but make it easier to be wise
Though now it seems impossible, and so
All that you need is patience."
Heart cries, "No,
I have not a crumb of comfort, not a grain.
Time can but make her beauty over again:
Because of that great nobleness of hers
The fire that stirs about her, when she stirs,
Burns but more clearly. O she had not these ways
When all the wild Summer was in her gaze."
Heart! O heart! if she'd but turn her head,
You'd know the folly of being comforted.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "The Folly Of Being Comforted"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
